behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on a real vision model correctly classifying explicit content. A mocked classifier can hide true-positive/false-negative behavior and threshold effects, so a pure Integration test can miss real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated explicit image set through the moderation endpoint using the production vision model configuration; assert each submission is marked flagged, routed to human-review state, and not published in the post store/feed.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model-quality behavior under adversarial text variants. Mocked NLP outputs would trivially pass and miss real robustness failures against obfuscation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real-model moderation test suite of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, inserted punctuation/spacing) through the live text-classification path; assert all variants are flagged and held for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is orchestration and state transition across moderation modules and persistence/publish wiring. A unit test of decision logic alone can miss real dropped-message or wrong-state integration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, queue/DB, and publisher in a local integration environment with deterministic allow responses from both classifiers; submit a post and assert final persisted state is published, with no review hold and no drop.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO across multiple AI moderation steps plus decisioning. Real timing risk comes from sequential model calls and pipeline composition; lower-level tests with mocks can hide true latency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions with real vision and text moderation calls under representative peak-hour load; measure p95/p99 decision latency from submission to final decision and assert completion <= 5 seconds for the required percentile target.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure handling depends on real external-call failure modes (timeouts, connection errors, 5xx) and queue behavior. In-process mocks can miss transport-level and retry-enqueue integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision dependency outage (e.g., blackhole endpoint or return repeated 5xx/timeouts) in a system test; submit image posts and assert they are enqueued with retry metadata/backoff and never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This requires repeated real model-involved decisions over time and verification of cross-request consistency policy (e.g., cache/stickiness). A single-call Agentic test can miss sequence-level inconsistency failures.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image N times within the configured short window through the full moderation service using the real vision model; assert decisions are identical across submissions and follow the system’s consistency mechanism (cached or deterministic policy).
